AccountSid.description:
Twilio-format AccountSid: literal "AC" + 32 hex chars (34
total). Server-generated at account creation; the same value
the customer's Twilio SDK validates in its constructor.
AccountSid.name: AccountSid

ApplicationSid.description:
Twilio-compatible Application SID — literal "AP" + 32 hex chars
(34 total). Server-generated on `POST /Applications`.
ApplicationSid.name: ApplicationSid

ConferenceSid.description:
Twilio-compatible Conference SID — literal `CF` + 32 hex chars
(34 total). Server-generated lazily on first
`<Dial><Conference>` dispatch (or on first appearance in
a `GET /Conferences` list); cleared when the conference
ends. The v0.19.x namespaced form
(`<AccountSid>__<friendly>`) is still accepted on input
for migration safety, but the response always emits the
CF shape.
ConferenceSid.name: ConferenceSid

PageToken.description: The page token. This is provided by the API.
PageToken.name: PageToken

PhoneNumber.description:
Legacy E.164 path form for IncomingPhoneNumber resources.
New code SHOULD use the canonical `PhoneNumberSid` parameter
(the `PN`-prefixed sid emitted on response). Accepted during
the v0.5.x soft-transition window for backwards-compat with
clients that still construct URLs from the E.164.
PhoneNumber.name: PhoneNumber

PhoneNumberSid.description:
Twilio-compatible IncomingPhoneNumber identifier — literal `PN` +
32 lowercase hex characters (34 total). Server-generated when
the DID is first claimed by an account; stable for the row's
lifetime on this account. The legacy E.164 path shape
(`/IncomingPhoneNumbers/+18005551234`) is still accepted on
input during the v0.5.x soft-transition window — same
precedent as `ConferenceSid` which accepts both canonical and
legacy forms. Responses always emit the `PN…` form on the
`sid` field regardless of which key resolved the request.
PhoneNumberSid.name: PhoneNumberSid

QueueSid.description:
Twilio-compatible Queue SID — literal "QU" + 32 hex chars (34
total). Server-generated on `POST /Queues`.
QueueSid.name: QueueSid

ResourceSid.description:
Twilio generic resource identifier on fetch-by-id endpoints
(`CA`/`RE`/`AP`/`QU`/`CF`/`PN` + 32 hex, or dashed `CA`
form). Parameter name is always `Sid` per twilio-oai; the
path segment position determines which prefix is valid.
ResourceSid.name: Sid

SipCredentialListSid.description:
Twilio-compatible SIP CredentialList SID — literal "CL" + 32 hex
chars (34 total). Server-generated on `POST /SIP/CredentialLists`.
Also names the parent list in the Credentials sub-resource path.
SipCredentialListSid.name: Sid

SipCredentialSid.description:
Twilio-compatible SIP Credential SID — literal "CR" + 32 hex
chars (34 total). Server-generated on
`POST /SIP/CredentialLists/{CredentialListSid}/Credentials`.
SipCredentialSid.name: Sid

SipDomainSid.description:
Twilio-compatible SIP Domain SID — literal "SD" + 32 hex chars
(34 total). Server-generated on `POST /SIP/Domains`.
SipDomainSid.name: Sid

SipIpAccessControlListSid.description:
Twilio-compatible SIP IpAccessControlList SID — literal "AL" +
32 hex chars (34 total). Server-generated on
`POST /SIP/IpAccessControlLists`. Also names the parent list in
the IpAddresses sub-resource path.
SipIpAccessControlListSid.name: Sid

SipIpAddressSid.description:
Twilio-compatible SIP IpAddress SID — literal "IP" + 32 hex
chars (34 total). Server-generated on
`POST /SIP/IpAccessControlLists/{IpAccessControlListSid}/IpAddresses`.
SipIpAddressSid.name: Sid

SipMappingListSid.description:
The mapped list's SID on a SIP Domain mapping leaf. A
CredentialList mapping carries a `CL…` sid; an
IpAccessControlList mapping carries an `AL…` sid. The mapping's
own `sid` IS the mapped list's sid (Twilio semantics).
SipMappingListSid.name: Sid

SipParentCredentialListSid.description:
Twilio-compatible SIP CredentialList SID — literal "CL" + 32 hex
chars (34 total). Names the parent CredentialList in the
Credentials sub-resource path. Matches Twilio's
`CredentialListSid` path-parameter name.
SipParentCredentialListSid.name: CredentialListSid

SipParentDomainSid.description:
Twilio-compatible SIP Domain SID — literal "SD" + 32 hex chars
(34 total). Names the parent Domain in a Domain mapping
sub-resource path. Matches Twilio's `DomainSid` path-parameter
name for the nested mapping surfaces.
SipParentDomainSid.name: DomainSid

SipParentIpAccessControlListSid.description:
Twilio-compatible SIP IpAccessControlList SID — literal "AL" +
32 hex chars (34 total). Names the parent IpAccessControlList in
the IpAddresses sub-resource path. Matches Twilio's
`IpAccessControlListSid` path-parameter name.
SipParentIpAccessControlListSid.name: IpAccessControlListSid

SubresourceSid.description:
Twilio-compatible subresource SID — used by Streams (`MZ` prefix),
Siprec (`SR` prefix), and Transcriptions (`RT` prefix). The
regex covers all three subresources mounted under
`/Calls/{CallSid}/{Streams|Siprec|Transcriptions}/{Sid}`.
SubresourceSid.name: Sid

BadRequest.description: Request validation failed (invalid phone number, malformed body, ...).

NotFound.description: The requested resource does not exist.

Unauthorized.description:
Authentication failed. All of the following return the same 401
with the same body, intentionally indistinguishable from outside:
  * Unknown :AccountSid
  * Disabled account
  * Wrong api_key
  * Source IP not in the account's allow_cidr (per-tenant allowlist)

voice_fallback_url.description: Fetched when voice_url returns non-2xx or fails.

answered_by.description:
AMD verdict. Wire-strict Twilio enum, suitable for
strict-mode SDK deserialisers.

caller_name.description: SIP From: display-name captured on inbound calls (CNAM-style identification). Empty for outbound originates and for inbound calls without a display name.

forwarded_from.description: Originator of a forwarded inbound call, captured from the SIP `Diversion` (RFC 5806) or `History-Info` (RFC 7044) header. Empty for non-forwarded calls.

from_formatted.description: Human-readable national formatting of `from`. Same generation rules as `to_formatted`.

parent_call_sid.description: Set on B-leg child calls (e.g. legs created by `<Dial>`). Empty on the originating A-leg or when no `<Dial>`-style bridge has occurred.

price.description: Twilio compatibility — always empty (VoiceML does not bill per-call).

queue_time.description: Seconds spent in `<Enqueue>` queue, as decimal string. Empty for non-queued calls.

to_formatted.description: Human-readable national formatting of `to`, e.g. `(800) 555-1234` for US/CA numbers. Generated via libphonenumber; falls back to the E.164 form if parsing fails.

CallPayment.description:
<Pay> REST companion resource. Twilio's response shape
is intentionally minimal — runtime config (chargeAmount,
paymentConnector, validCardTypes, etc.) is captured server-side
but not echoed back. VoiceML tracks the status enum locally
so SDKs can introspect lifecycle progression; the field is
surfaced as a VoiceML extension on top of Twilio's response
schema.

CallTranscription.description:
Live per-call transcription resource (REST equivalent of
`<Start><Transcription>`). Distinct from any post-recording
transcription resource — events are streamed via the
StatusCallback URL while the call is live.

call_sid_ending_conference.description: Populated only when a participant left with `endConferenceOnExit=true`; empty otherwise.

date_created.description: Emitted via omitempty when not tracked (room state is queried live; creation timestamps are not persisted).

member_count.description: VoiceML extension — count of current participants.

reason_conference_ended.description:
Populated only on the synthesised post-end response —
e.g. `conference-ended-via-rest-api` after a
`Status=completed` POST. Empty while in-progress.

region.description:
Twilio edge region label. Defaults to `us1`; reserved
for operator-overridable region taxonomy in a future
release. Informational — VoiceML does not route
by region today.

status.description:
`init` matches Twilio's transient pre-mix-engine state. VoiceML
never emits it today (the conference materializes only after a
participant joins), but the value is documented for SDK
enum-deserializer compatibility.

ApplicationSid.description:
References a stored Application resource. Its `VoiceUrl` /
`VoiceMethod` / `VoiceFallbackUrl` / `VoiceFallbackMethod` /
`StatusCallback{,Method,Event}` fields fill any per-call
request field that's empty. Per-call values always win —
the Application provides defaults, not overrides.
Cross-tenant Application lookups return 404 with the same
shape as a nonexistent sid.

AsyncAmd.description: Twilio compatibility — AMD on VoiceML is always async; this flag is accepted but redundant.

Byoc.description: Twilio BYOC Trunk SID. Accepted-and-ignored — VoiceML routes via gateways configured at deploy time.

CallReason.description: Stored as a custom channel variable on the originating call leg, accessible to customer dialplans for reporting and routing decisions.

CallToken.description: Twilio CallToken for verified-caller-id flows. Accepted-and-ignored.

CallerId.description: Alternate to `From` for SIP From: rewriting. When set, drives the channel `effective_caller_id_number`.

FallbackUrl.description:
URL to fetch TwiML from if the primary `Url` / `Twiml`
source fails to fetch or parse. Tried only on primary
failure.

Method.description: HTTP method used to fetch `Url` (Twilio default = POST).

RecordingChannels.description: Recording channel layout. Accepted-and-mono-only in v0.19.x — `dual` is parsed but the writer emits a single mixed channel.

RecordingTrack.description: Accepted-and-ignored in v0.19.x — recording captures the bridged audio regardless.

SendDigits.description: DTMF burst played after answer — charset `0-9*#wW` (`w`=500ms, `W`=1s pause). Wired into the originate.

SipAuthPassword.description: SIP digest auth password (paired with SipAuthUsername).

SipAuthUsername.description: SIP digest auth username for BYOC trunks; channel var `sip_auth_username`.

Trim.description: Accepted-and-ignored in v0.19.x — recording is fixed at `trim-silence`.

Twiml.description:
Inline TwiML body — alternative to `Url`. If both are set,
`Twiml` wins (matches Twilio's documented precedence).

Url.description:
HTTP(S) endpoint that returns TwiML when the call answers.
Mutually exclusive with `Twiml` (inline body wins if both set).

detail.description: Human-readable error from the underlying check, including the value-vs-threshold context where relevant.

IncomingPhoneNumber.description:
Tenant-self-serve view of an assigned DID. Twilio-compatible —
`sid` is the canonical `PN`-prefixed opaque identifier (34 chars)
and `phone_number` carries the E.164 form. These are distinct
fields.

The full Twilio field set is documented here so strict-binding
SDKs deserialize without throwing. Fields VoiceML doesn't track
(regulatory, SMS, emergency, trunking) emit a Twilio-compat
default — empty string, `null`, or the documented enum default.
Customer code that reads e.g. `number.capabilities.voice`
therefore works with the same SDK patterns on VoiceML and Twilio.

address_requirements.description: Twilio-compatible: address requirement class. VoiceML emits `none`.

address_sid.description: Twilio-compatible: regulatory street-address resource. Not tracked by VoiceML — empty.

beta.description: Twilio-compatible: `true` for numbers from beta-availability inventory. VoiceML always emits `false`.

bundle_sid.description: Twilio-compatible: regulatory bundle. Not tracked — empty.

capabilities.description: Twilio-compatible: which channel types this DID supports. VoiceML is voice-only — `voice=true`, the rest `false`.

emergency_address_sid.description: Twilio-compatible: E911 address resource. Not tracked — empty.

emergency_address_status.description: Twilio-compatible: E911 registration state. Not tracked — empty.

emergency_status.description: Twilio-compatible: E911 registration. Not handled by VoiceML — emits empty.

friendly_name.description: Display name. Empty by default in v0.5.x; tenant-editable via POST in a future release.

identity_sid.description: Twilio-compatible: business-identity resource. Not tracked — empty.

origin.description: Twilio-compatible: source of the number. VoiceML emits empty (provisioning model is operator-managed, not number-buy).

sms_url.description: Twilio-compatible: SMS handler URL. VoiceML is voice-only — empty.

status.description: Twilio-compatible: provisioning status (`in-use`, etc.). VoiceML doesn't model this — empty.

status_callback.description: Twilio-compatible: lifecycle webhook. VoiceML emits empty (status is per-call via Call status_callback).

trunk_sid.description: Twilio-compatible: SIP trunk routing. VoiceML uses gateway-config-driven trunking — emits empty.

type.description: Twilio-compatible: number class. VoiceML doesn't categorize numbers this way — emits empty.

uri.description: Path under `/2010-04-01/Accounts/{AccountSid}/IncomingPhoneNumbers/{Sid}.json` — uses the canonical PN sid form.

voice_application_sid.description: Twilio-compatible: linked `AP…` Application sid that supplies voice_url. VoiceML's `<Dial><Application>` flow keeps the link on the Application resource, not here — emits empty.

voice_caller_id_lookup.description: Twilio-compatible: CNAM lookup flag. VoiceML doesn't perform CNAM — always `false`.

voice_receive_mode.description: Twilio-compatible: VoiceML routes inbound as voice only — always `voice`.

body.description: Message text. Empty string after a redaction update.

error_code.description: Twilio-shape error taxonomy. 21609 = gateway not configured; 30001 = upstream gateway failure.

error_message.description: Sanitised — never contains upstream URL or credentials.

from.description: Source phone number; falls back to the tenant's configured default sender if omitted.

num_segments.description: Per-message segment count as Twilio surfaces it — string-typed wire format.

to.description: Destination phone number in E.164 form.

call_sid_to_coach.description:
When `coaching` is true, the CallSid of the participant this
coach can speak to (Twilio whisper-coach). Empty when
`coaching` is false or the coach target has not joined.

coaching.description:
True when this participant joined with the `coach` role
(Twilio whisper-coach pattern). A coach hears the entire
conference but is only audible to the participant
identified by `call_sid_to_coach`.

date_created.description: Emitted via omitempty when not tracked.

date_updated.description: Emitted via omitempty when not tracked.

end_conference_on_exit.description: Reflects the `<Dial><Conference>` attribute used at join time. v0.19.x emits the documented default (`false`) for every participant.

label.description: Optional tag set via `<Conference participantLabel=…>`. Empty when not set.

queue_time.description:
Pre-join queue-wait in seconds, string-encoded per
Twilio's wire shape. "0" when the participant did not
transit a queue (the common case). Populated from
`<Enqueue>` dequeue events.

start_conference_on_enter.description: Reflects the `<Dial><Conference>` attribute used at join time. v0.19.x emits the documented default (`true`) for every participant — per-participant attribute persistence is on the roadmap.

status.description:
Derived from live conference state: `connected`
while the participant is in the room, `on-hold` while
the `hold` flag is set, `completed` after they've
left (visible only briefly via the synthesised
response when a mute/hold update races a leave).

average_wait_time.description: Seconds in the trailing 5-minute window. 0 when no recent exits.

max_size.description: Capacity ceiling; 0 means unlimited (Twilio default). Further enqueues fail when current_size equals a positive max_size.

conference_sid.description: Set only on conference-room captures (`<Dial><Conference record="record-from-start">`); empty on per-leg recordings.

encryption_details.description: Reserved for SSE-KMS metadata when encryption details are exposed; currently empty.

error_code.description:
Twilio-compatible per-call error code for failed
recordings. `null` (the common case) means no error;
non-zero values reference twilio.com/docs/api/errors.
The wire shape uses `null`, never `0` — strict-mode SDK
deserialisers expect null on the no-error path.

media_url.description:
Twilio-compatible URL for the recording audio.
VoiceML serves the WAV at `/Accounts/{AccountSid}/Recordings/{Sid}.wav`
— emitted here so strict-binding SDK clients (e.g.
twilio-csharp's `recording.MediaUrl`) bind cleanly without
null-deref. Same auth requirements as the resource itself.

status.description:
Twilio-compatible enum. `failed` is intentionally absent — Twilio
does not document it on Recording.status (failure of an Outbound
recording surfaces as `absent`). VoiceML production code only
emits `completed`; the wider set is documented for SDK compatibility.

RecordingList.description:
Canonical Twilio pagination envelope for recording-list
endpoints. Used by both the account-scoped `/Recordings`
list, the per-call `/Calls/{Sid}/Recordings` list, and the
per-conference `/Conferences/{Sid}/Recordings` list — every
SDK auto-paginator depends on the same fields.

auth_type.description: Authentication scheme bound to the domain (e.g. via CredentialList / IpAccessControlList mappings). Empty when no auth mapping is attached.

domain_name.description: Fully-qualified SIP domain. Required on create.

domain_sid.description: The SipDomain resource this mapping is attached to.

cidr_prefix_length.description: CIDR prefix length. Defaults to 32 (single host) when omitted on create.

connector_name.description: SIPREC profile name. Falls back to the configured default when empty.

date_created.description: Set on start; preserved across update.

name.description: Operator-supplied label for the stream; round-trips on the resource.

UpdateCallRequest.description:
POST /Calls/{Sid} body. The endpoint serves four intents:

* `Status=completed|canceled` — terminate the live call.
* `Twiml=<inline>` — execute inline TwiML on the live call;
  wins over `Url` when both are present (matches Twilio).
* `Url=…` (`Method`/`FallbackUrl`/`FallbackMethod`) — fetch
  TwiML and execute on the live call. Falls through to
  `FallbackUrl` on primary fetch/parse failure.
* `StatusCallback*` — swap the live call's lifecycle-
  callback wiring (URL / method / event-mask). Twilio
  "only-set fields" precedence: empty fields preserve the
  previous value; absent `StatusCallbackEvent` leaves the
  existing event mask, present (one or more) replaces.

Precedence: Status wins over any TwiML source. The
StatusCallback* update is independent and applies on every
request that carries it (including the terminate path).

StatusCallback.description: Swaps the live call's StatusCallback URL.

StatusCallbackEvent.description: Replaces the event-mask. Repeatable form param; empty list defaults to `[completed]` (Twilio default).

StatusCallbackMethod.description: Swaps the live call's StatusCallback HTTP method.

basicAuth.description:
Username MUST equal the URL `:AccountSid` path parameter.
Password is the per-tenant API key set via the admin API.

info.description:
VoiceML exposes a Twilio-compatible REST + TwiML surface covering
voice (inbound + outbound calls, conferences, queues), SMS via the
Messages resource, mid-call recording / SIPREC / real-time
transcription / `<Pay>` subresources, and the `IncomingPhoneNumbers`
+ `Applications` configuration surfaces. Existing Twilio SDKs
(`twilio-node`, `twilio-python`, `twilio-go`, ...) integrate with
minimal migration effort. Authentication, error codes, and
pagination follow Twilio's documented conventions where supported.

URL format: Twilio's canonical URL form ends with `.json` (e.g.
`/Calls/{Sid}.json`). VoiceML accepts BOTH the `.json`-suffixed and
the unsuffixed form — the server strips a trailing `.json`/`.xml`/`.wav`
suffix before routing (see `WrapWithFormatSuffix`). The spec documents the
`.json` form so SDKs generated against this spec produce
Twilio-compatible URLs out of the box; existing clients that omit `.json`
continue to work unchanged.

Authentication is per-tenant HTTP Basic:
  * Username = `:AccountSid` (the URL path parameter, Twilio-format
    AC + 32 hex chars). Same value the Twilio SDK validates in its
    constructor — migration-compatible.
  * Password = the per-account API key.
The same key is used to compute `X-Twilio-Signature` on outbound webhooks.

Account management (create / rotate / disable / delete) is NOT exposed on
this public listener — it lives on a private observability listener
documented separately.
info.summary: Twilio-compatible voice (inbound + outbound), SMS, conferencing, recording, transcription, and payment platform
info.title: VoiceML — Twilio-compatible REST API

parameters.description: Filter to Applications with this exact FriendlyName.
parameters.name: FriendlyName

get.summary: List Applications for the account (paged, FriendlyName ASC)

post.description:
Persistent named TwiML+callback bundle — referenced by
`<Dial><Application>AP…</Application></Dial>`. The agent's
Dial verb resolves the SID, fetches voice_url, and runs the
returned TwiML on the call leg.
post.summary: Create an Application

get.summary: Fetch an Application

post.summary: Update an Application (partial — only set fields are touched)

get.description:
Paginated list of call records for the given account. Filters
match Twilio: `To`, `From`, `Status`, `ParentCallSid`,
`StartTime` (bare = full UTC day), `StartTime<` (strict upper),
`StartTime>` (strict lower), and the same triple for `EndTime`.
For backwards compatibility the legacy `StartTime>=` / `EndTime<=`
SQL-style operators are also accepted at the handler. Pagination
via `Page` and `PageSize` (max 1000). `EndTime` filters
restrict the result set to terminal-status rows (an in-progress
call has no end time and cannot match an EndTime window).

parameters.description: Calls started on this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD).
parameters.name: StartTime

parameters.description: Calls started strictly before this UTC date/time.
parameters.name: StartTime<

parameters.description: Calls started strictly after this UTC date/time.
parameters.name: StartTime>

parameters.description: Calls ended on this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD).
parameters.name: EndTime

parameters.description: Calls ended strictly before this UTC date/time.
parameters.name: EndTime<

parameters.description: Calls ended strictly after this UTC date/time.
parameters.name: EndTime>

get.summary: List calls

post.description:
Originates an outbound call. The `To` and `From`
numbers are validated and normalised to E.164 against the
authenticated tenant's `phone_regions` list — set per-account in
the admin API. Empty list disables validation; multi-region
tenants accept numbers valid in any listed region.
`MachineDetection` is optional; omitting it skips AMD entirely.
post.summary: Create (originate) a call

get.description:
Twilio's fine-grained per-call event stream. VoiceML
delivers equivalent data via `StatusCallback` (initiated /
ringing / answered / completed); this endpoint is mounted
as a stub returning an empty list so SDK helpers iterate
cleanly. The canonical event source is the customer's
`StatusCallback` URL. Page/PageSize are accepted for SDK
ergonomics but have no effect on an empty list.
get.summary: List per-call lifecycle events (compat stub)

get.description:
Twilio's per-call alert log. VoiceML doesn't track
notifications as a separate stream — relevant operator-side
events flow through metrics + structured logs. This
endpoint is mounted as a stub so SDK helpers don't 404;
the response is always an empty list. Page/PageSize and Log
are accepted for SDK ergonomics but have no effect on an
empty list.

parameters.description:
Twilio-compatible parameter — 0 = error-level only, 1 =
warning-level + above. Accepted for SDK migration
compatibility; has no effect since VoiceML's per-call
notification stream is unimplemented (this endpoint is a
stub returning an empty list).
parameters.name: Log

parameters.description: Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on empty notification stub.
parameters.name: MessageDate

parameters.description: Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on empty notification stub.
parameters.name: MessageDate<

parameters.description: Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on empty notification stub.
parameters.name: MessageDate>

get.summary: List per-call notifications (compat stub)

get.description:
Per-call notification fetch. VoiceML does not persist a
notification stream — the list endpoint is always empty and
individual fetches return 404.
get.summary: Fetch a per-call notification (compat stub)

post.description:
Initiates a PCI Pay session against the live call leg. Mirrors
the TwiML `<Pay>` verb's parser + state machine; every
attribute below is accepted and validated. The runtime
(Stripe charge / tokenize) is currently gated; until it is
wired, both surfaces accept and log without invoking the
Stripe connector.

Per-tenant BYO is binding: the tenant's account row must have
`pay_enabled = true` AND `stripe_secret_key` set (admin
opt-in). No server-level Stripe key fallback — multi-tenancy
demands no fallback.

`IdempotencyKey` is accepted and persisted on the row for
diagnostic visibility; replay dedup is NOT enforced today.

IdempotencyKey.description: Body-level dedup token. Accepted; replay dedup not enforced.

Parameter.description: Single-level JSON object passed to the payment connector.

ValidCardTypes.description: Space-separated. Default: visa mastercard amex maestro discover optima jcb diners-club enroute.

403.description: Account is not pay_enabled or has no stripe_secret_key.

post.summary: Start a <Pay> session on an existing call (REST equivalent of `<Pay>` TwiML)

202.description: Pay session update accepted; final settlement may still be pending.

post.summary: Update a Pay session — Status=complete or Status=cancel

parameters.description: Filter to recordings created on this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD).
parameters.name: DateCreated

parameters.description: Filter to recordings created strictly before this UTC date.
parameters.name: DateCreated<

parameters.description: Filter to recordings created strictly after this UTC date.
parameters.name: DateCreated>

get.summary: List recordings for one call

post.description:
Begins a fresh recording on the live call leg. The new
Recording resource is returned in the 201 response. Distinct
from `Record=true` on `POST /Calls`, which captures from the
start of the call.

RecordingMaxDuration.description: Cap recording length in seconds. 0 = no limit.

201.description:
Recording started (Twilio-historical 201 Created). VoiceML
returns 200 OK exclusively; this code is declared so the
spec matches twilio-oai's documented response set verbatim
and SDK codegen produces the same response-handling
switch. Aligned with the Twilio OpenAPI reference response set.

post.summary: Start a recording mid-call

delete.description:
Removes the memdb row, unlinks the on-disk WAV, and (when
`RECORDING_S3_BUCKET` is configured) issues an S3 DeleteObject
on the archived copy. S3 deletion is best-effort: failure logs
but does not fail the API call (the bucket lifecycle policy is
the long-stop cleanup). Tenant-scoped — cross-tenant DELETE
returns 404 without affecting the row.
delete.summary: Delete a recording (row + local WAV + S3 object when present)

get.description:
Same JSON resource as `/Recordings/{RecordingSid}` but enforces
the call-scope from the URL path. 404 if the recording exists
but is attached to a different call.
get.summary: Fetch recording metadata for a specific call (call-scoped form)

post.description:
Applies stop / pause / resume on the live recording.
All three transitions return 200 + the post-mutation
Recording resource. Tenant-scoped: a RecordingSid that
belongs to a different account returns 404 without leaking
existence.

400.description: Pause / resume failed against the live call.

408.description:
Twilio returns 408 Request Timeout when the recording is locked.
VoiceML uses 503/409 instead — both are declared for SDK compatibility.

post.summary: Update a mid-call recording's status

get.description:
Returns 0 or 1 entries — only one SIPREC mirror per call leg
is supported. Pagination envelope is the Twilio-compatible
shape.

200.description: Page of SIPREC sessions (0 or 1 entry).

get.summary: List active SIPREC sessions for the call

post.description:
Starts a SIPREC mirror against the configured connector
profile on the live call leg. Returns the new Siprec
resource with an SR-prefixed sid.

ConnectorName.description: SIPREC profile name. Empty falls back to the configured default profile.

post.summary: Start a SIPREC session on the call (REST equivalent of `<Start><Siprec>`)

get.summary: Fetch a single SIPREC session by sid

post.description:
The SIPREC integration doesn't expose a stop verb; the SRS
recording session continues until the call hangs up. This
endpoint clears VoiceML's session-tracking entry only.
post.summary: Update a SIPREC session — clears tracking only

get.description:
Returns 0 or 1 entries — the runtime supports a single
active media fork per call, matching the slot model on the
VoiceML tracker. Pagination envelope is the Twilio-compatible
shape so SDK auto-paginators round-trip cleanly.
get.summary: List active media streams for the call

post.description:
Starts a media fork on the live call leg. Equivalent to
`<Connect><Stream>` / `<Start><Stream>` in TwiML. Returns
the new Stream resource with an MZ-prefixed sid.

Url.description: wss:// endpoint for the customer's WebSocket server. Required.

post.summary: Start a media stream on the call (REST equivalent of `<Connect><Stream>`/`<Start><Stream>`)

get.summary: Fetch a single stream by sid

post.summary: Update a media stream — only Status=stopped is meaningful

get.description:
Returns 0 or 1 entries. Pagination envelope is the Twilio-compatible
shape.
get.summary: List active transcriptions for the call

post.summary: Start real-time transcription on the call (REST equivalent of `<Start><Transcription>`)

get.summary: Fetch a single transcription by sid

post.summary: Update a transcription — only Status=stopped is meaningful

post.description:
Twilio's chat-style messages on a live call. Real
message-passing infrastructure (a websocket on the
customer-facing leg) that's out of scope for VoiceML.
Returns 501 with Twilio code 20501. The empty request body
schema is declared so codegen tooling emits a method
signature consistent with Twilio's SDK.

201.description:
Twilio declares 201 Created on success. VoiceML returns 501
Not Implemented — both are declared for SDK codegen compatibility.

post.summary: Send a user-defined message on a live call (NOT IMPLEMENTED)

get.summary: Fetch a call

post.description:
Three flows on the same endpoint (mirrors Twilio):
  * `Status=completed|canceled` — terminate the call.
  * `Twiml=<inline>` — execute inline TwiML on the live call
    (wins over `Url` when both are present).
  * `Url=…` — fetch new TwiML and execute it on the live call
    (live redirect / hot swap).
If `Status` is present alongside any TwiML source, `Status`
wins. The success response is the full Call resource — same
shape `GET` returns — so SDK deserialisers round-trip.
post.summary: Update or terminate a live call

get.description:
Returns conferences currently active for this tenant. Live
query — no caching, always reflects state at request time.
Conferences appear once a first participant joins (typically
via TwiML `<Dial><Conference>`) and disappear as soon as the
last participant leaves.

parameters.description: Filter to conferences with this exact FriendlyName.
parameters.name: FriendlyName

parameters.description: Filter to conferences in this lifecycle state.
parameters.name: Status

parameters.description: Twilio-compatible parameter. VoiceML's conference list is a live query with no historical store — accepted but does not narrow the result set.
parameters.name: DateCreated

parameters.description: Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on live conference list.
parameters.name: DateCreated<

parameters.description: Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on live conference list.
parameters.name: DateCreated>

parameters.description: Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on live conference list.
parameters.name: DateUpdated

parameters.description: Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on live conference list.
parameters.name: DateUpdated<

parameters.description: Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on live conference list.
parameters.name: DateUpdated>

503.description: Conference subsystem not configured (passive AMD-on-fork mode).

get.summary: List active conferences for the account

parameters.description: Filter to participants with this Muted state.
parameters.name: Muted

parameters.description: Filter to participants with this Hold state.
parameters.name: Hold

parameters.description: Filter to participants with this Coaching state.
parameters.name: Coaching

get.summary: List participants in a conference

post.description:
Places an outbound call from `From` to `To` that joins the named
conference on answer via inline TwiML. Returns immediately with
`status=queued` while the leg is ringing.

From.description: Caller ID / originating endpoint (E.164 or Twilio client:/sip:/app: form).

To.description: Destination endpoint to dial into the conference.

503.description: Conference or outbound dial subsystem not configured.

post.summary: Dial a participant into a conference

delete.summary: Kick a participant out of the conference

get.summary: Fetch a single participant

post.description:
At least one of `Muted` or `Hold` must be set. Twilio's
`HoldUrl` (custom hold-music URL) and `AnnounceUrl` are
not yet implemented.

Hold.description: Place the participant on hold (default hold-music profile) or remove from hold.

post.summary: Mute / unmute / hold a participant

get.description:
Returns recordings created by `<Dial><Conference record="record-from-start">`
verbs that ran in this conference. Pagination envelope same as
`/Recordings`.

parameters.description: Filter to recordings created on this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD).
parameters.name: DateCreated

parameters.description: Filter to recordings created strictly before this UTC date.
parameters.name: DateCreated<

parameters.description: Filter to recordings created strictly after this UTC date.
parameters.name: DateCreated>

get.summary: List recordings captured by a conference

delete.description:
Same semantics as `/Recordings/{Sid}` but enforces conference
scope from the URL path.

get.description:
Same JSON resource as `/Recordings/{RecordingSid}` but enforces
the conference scope from the URL path. 404 if the recording exists
but is attached to a different conference.
get.summary: Fetch a conference-scoped recording

post.description:
Same semantics as `/Calls/{CallSid}/Recordings/{Sid}` but enforces
conference scope from the URL path.
post.summary: Update a conference-scoped recording's status

get.summary: Fetch a single conference

post.description:
v1 supports only `Status=completed` — kicks every participant
and tears down the room. Twilio's other update fields
(`Announce*`) are not yet implemented.

503.description: Conference subsystem not configured (passive AMD-on-fork mode).

post.summary: End an active conference

get.description:
Returns every DID currently bound to the authenticated
account. Tenant-scoped — only the caller's own rows are
returned; the global admin list endpoint is on the private
listener and not described here.

parameters.description:
Exact-match filter on the E.164 phone number. Twilio's
canonical lookup pattern is
`GET /IncomingPhoneNumbers?PhoneNumber=+1...` followed by
`GET /IncomingPhoneNumbers/{sid}` using the `sid` from the
list result. Twilio-compatible SDK clients issue this
pattern; the filter is supported so the same call pattern
works against VoiceML. Returns a 0-or-1-row envelope.
parameters.name: PhoneNumber

parameters.description:
Twilio-compatible parameter. VoiceML doesn't model per-DID
FriendlyName on phone_numbers (the field is empty in every
view), so this parameter is accepted but does not narrow
or widen the result set. Declared for SDK migration
compatibility.
parameters.name: FriendlyName

parameters.description:
Twilio-compatible parameter. VoiceML does not model the
carrier-supplied "beta" flag (no upstream beta-status
distinction), so this parameter is accepted but does not
narrow or widen the result set. Declared for SDK migration
compatibility.
parameters.name: Beta

parameters.description:
Twilio-compatible parameter. VoiceML treats every assigned DID
as locally provisioned (no twilio/hosted origin
distinction), so this parameter is accepted but does not
narrow or widen the result set. Declared for SDK migration
compatibility.
parameters.name: Origin

get.summary: List DIDs assigned to the authenticated tenant

post.description:
Tenant self-serve assignment of an E.164 DID. Idempotent on
the same tenant — re-POSTing the same `PhoneNumber` rebinds
its voice routing without erroring (matches Twilio's update
semantics). Returns 409 when the number is already claimed
by a different account.

200.description: Existing DID rebound to the same tenant (idempotent re-POST).

409.description: Phone number is already assigned to a different account.

post.summary: Assign a DID to the authenticated tenant

get.description:
Twilio type-specific list alias. Returns DIDs inferred as
`local` (non-toll-free US numbers and all non-US numbers).
Same pagination envelope as `/IncomingPhoneNumbers`.

200.description: Page of local incoming phone numbers.

get.summary: List local DIDs assigned to the authenticated tenant

post.description:
Twilio type-specific create alias — same semantics as
`POST /IncomingPhoneNumbers`.

409.description: Phone number is already assigned to a different account.

post.summary: Assign a local DID to the authenticated tenant

get.description:
Twilio type-specific list alias. VoiceML does not track carrier
mobile classification on BYO DIDs — this endpoint returns an
empty page unless future metadata is added.

200.description: Page of mobile incoming phone numbers.

get.summary: List mobile DIDs assigned to the authenticated tenant

post.description:
Twilio type-specific create alias — same semantics as
`POST /IncomingPhoneNumbers`.

409.description: Phone number is already assigned to a different account.

post.summary: Assign a mobile DID to the authenticated tenant

get.description:
Twilio type-specific list alias. Returns US toll-free NPAs
(800/833/844/855/866/877/888). Same pagination envelope as
`/IncomingPhoneNumbers`.

200.description: Page of toll-free incoming phone numbers.

get.summary: List toll-free DIDs assigned to the authenticated tenant

post.description:
Twilio type-specific create alias — same semantics as
`POST /IncomingPhoneNumbers`.

409.description: Phone number is already assigned to a different account.

post.summary: Assign a toll-free DID to the authenticated tenant

delete.description:
Tenant-scoped — a number assigned to a different account 404s
without affecting the row. Idempotent: 204 on success OR if
the number was already gone.
delete.summary: Release a DID from the authenticated tenant

get.description:
Tenant-scoped — a phone number that exists but belongs to a
different account 404s with the same shape as a nonexistent
number (no enumeration leak).
get.summary: Fetch a single DID assigned to the authenticated tenant

post.description:
Only-set-fields-touched semantics. Tenant-scoped — a number
assigned to a different account 404s.
post.summary: Update voice routing on an assigned DID

get.summary: List Messages for the account (paged, DateCreated DESC)

post.description:
Sends `Body` to `To` via the configured SMS provider. When
the provider is not configured the row is still persisted
with status=failed and error_code=21609 so the integrator can
introspect the rejection through GET /Messages/{Sid}.

From.description: Source phone number; falls back to the tenant's configured default sender.

MessagingServiceSid.description: Accepted for parity; not yet routed against.

StatusCallback.description: Reserved — outbound SMS is fire-and-forget today.

post.summary: Send an SMS

get.summary: Fetch a Message

Body.description: Pass empty string to redact. Non-empty Body is ignored — Twilio's documented redaction semantics.

Status.description: Returns 21610 — outbound SMS is fire-and-forget, no cancel path.

409.description: Cancellation not supported by the SMS provider.

post.summary: Update a Message (Body redaction; Status=canceled rejected)

get.description:
Account-wide notification log. VoiceML delivers events via
StatusCallback — this endpoint returns an empty list so SDK
helpers do not 404.

parameters.description: Twilio-compatible date filter — accept-but-no-op on empty notification stub.

get.description: Always 404 — VoiceML does not persist notifications.
get.summary: Fetch an account notification (compat stub)
List queues for the account (paged, FriendlyName ASC)

post.description:
Create a named queue. Idempotent — POSTing the same
FriendlyName a second time returns the existing queue rather
than erroring (matches Twilio).
post.summary: Create a queue

get.description:
Returned ordered by position; position 1 = longest waiting
(front of queue).
get.summary: List waiting members in a queue
Peek the longest-waiting member

post.description:
Pops the longest-waiting member and redirects their call to the
URL in the body. The member's <Enqueue> wait-loop fetches that
URL and runs the returned TwiML on their leg as continuation.
post.summary: Dequeue the front member to a TwiML URL

get.summary: Fetch a specific member by CallSid

post.summary: Dequeue a specific member to a TwiML URL

delete.description:
Refused with 409 if any members are still waiting — caller must
drain the queue first (matches Twilio's behaviour).

get.summary: Fetch a queue

post.summary: Update a queue (FriendlyName / MaxSize)

parameters.description: Filter to recordings whose CallSid equals this value.
parameters.name: CallSid

parameters.description: Filter to recordings whose ConferenceSid equals this value.

parameters.description:
Twilio-compatible parameter. VoiceML hard-deletes recordings —
there is no soft-delete store — so this parameter is
accepted but does not change the result set.
parameters.name: IncludeSoftDeleted

get.summary: List recordings for the account

delete.description:
Removes the memdb row, unlinks the on-disk WAV, and (when
`RECORDING_S3_BUCKET` is configured) issues an S3 DeleteObject
on the archived copy. S3 deletion is best-effort: failure logs
but does not fail the API call (the bucket lifecycle policy is
the long-stop cleanup). Tenant-scoped — cross-tenant DELETE
returns 404 without affecting the row.
delete.summary: Delete a recording (row + local WAV + S3 object when present)

get.description:
Returns JSON metadata for the recording. The companion
`.wav` path returns the audio bytes (or a 302/410 redirect
depending on archival state); see `recordings.fetchAudio`.

Tenant-scoped: a RecordingSid that belongs to a different
account returns `404` with the same shape as a nonexistent
sid — no enumeration leak.

See [docs/aws-recordings.md](https://github.com/voicetel/callBroadcast/blob/main/docs/aws-recordings.md)
for the archival pipeline.

parameters.description:
Twilio-compatible parameter. VoiceML hard-deletes recordings —
accepted but does not change fetch behaviour.
parameters.name: IncludeSoftDeleted

get.summary: Fetch recording metadata

get.description:
Returns the captured audio as `audio/wav` with one of three
delivery shapes:

  * `200 OK` — local file present (typical for the first
    `RECORDING_LOCAL_GRACE` window after the call ends).
  * `302 Found` — local file cleaned up but archived to S3;
    `Location` header points at a presigned URL with TTL
    `RECORDING_PRESIGN_TTL`.
  * `410 Gone` — local file gone AND no `s3_key` set
    (typically: archival not configured + cleanup ran, or
    archival failed and the WAV was lost).

Tenant-scoped: a RecordingSid that belongs to a different
account returns `404` with the same shape as a nonexistent
sid — no enumeration leak.

See [docs/aws-recordings.md](https://github.com/voicetel/callBroadcast/blob/main/docs/aws-recordings.md)
for the archival pipeline.

302.description: WAV archived to S3; `Location` is a presigned URL.

410.description: Recording audio is no longer available (no local file, no S3 key).

get.summary: Fetch recording audio (WAV)

get.description:
Composite probe with two-tier severity. Hard checks (storage,
disk free space, voice stack processes + required modules +
SIP profile state) flip the LB-facing `ok` boolean. Soft
checks (host CPU loadavg, memory pressure, workload percents,
cluster health) surface in `warnings` only — the LB stays in
rotation. Result cached 5 s on full success.

200.description: All Hard checks pass. `warnings` may still be populated.

503.description: One or more Hard checks failed. Body lists failures + any concurrent warnings.

get.summary: Deep liveness probe

tags.description:
The Call resource and its non-recording subresources
(Notifications, Events, UserDefinedMessages). Twilio-parity tag.
tags.name: Api20100401Call

tags.description:
Recordings scoped to a parent call — list/start/fetch/update/delete
under `/Calls/{Sid}/Recordings`. Mirrors twilio-oai CallRecording.
tags.name: Api20100401CallRecording

tags.description:
Per-call notification list (compat stub; VoiceML's canonical event
source is the customer's StatusCallback URL).
tags.name: Api20100401CallNotification

tags.description:
Per-call transcription subresource — list/create/fetch/delete
under `/Calls/{Sid}/Transcriptions`. Twilio-parity tag.
tags.name: Api20100401CallTranscription

tags.description: The Conference resource — list/fetch/update. Twilio-parity tag.
tags.name: Api20100401Conference

tags.description:
Recordings scoped to a parent conference — list under
`/Conferences/{Sid}/Recordings`. Twilio-parity tag.
tags.name: Api20100401ConferenceRecording

tags.description:
Conference participants — list/fetch/update/kick under
`/Conferences/{Sid}/Participants`. Twilio-parity tag.

tags.description:
The Queue resource — create/list/fetch/update/delete.
Twilio-parity tag.

tags.description:
Queue members — list/peekFront/dequeueFront/fetch/dequeue under
`/Queues/{Sid}/Members`. Twilio-parity tag.

tags.description:
The Application resource — create/list/fetch/update/delete.

under `/SIP/CredentialLists`. Twilio-parity tag.

SIP Credentials scoped to a parent CredentialList —

mapped list's shape. Twilio-parity tag.

tags.description:
The Message resource — create/list/fetch/update/delete. VoiceML's
SMS surface translates Twilio-shape requests to the configured
SMS provider. Outbound-only today; inbound and MMS land in a
follow-up release. Twilio-parity tag.

Account-scoped recording catalog — list/fetch/delete + binary

<Pay> REST companion under `/Calls/{Sid}/Payments` — start /
modify a PCI Pay session against an existing call leg without
round-tripping TwiML. Gated by per-tenant
`pay_enabled` + `stripe_secret_key`.
Twilio-parity tag.

tags.description:
VoiceML extension: /health probe and /openapi.{yaml,yml,json}
self-publish endpoints. Not part of Twilio's surface; tagged
separately so SDK codegen can choose to skip these or emit them
under a non-Twilio module.
tags.name: Diagnostic
